ogTitle: Couta Rocks vs Crabtree: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Couta Rocks and Crabtree.
heading: Couta Rocks vs Crabtree — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Couta Rocks, Tasmania and Crabtree, Tasmania. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Crabtree has a lower overall climate risk score (31/100) compared to Couta Rocks (34/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Couta Rocks or Crabtree safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Couta Rocks has a flood risk score of 58/100, while Crabtree scores 23/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Couta Rocks scores 23/100 for bushfire risk. Crabtree scores 29/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Couta Rocks or Crabtree?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Crabtree has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.58% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
